text:
Admission Date:  2012-03-04              Discharge Date:   1993-05-20

Date of Birth:  2014-08-27             Sex:   M

Service: CARDIOTHORACIC

Allergies:
Lipitor

Attending:Heather
Chief Complaint:
Dyspnea and right shoulder pain

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
1. CABG x3 (LIMA-LAD, SVG-PDA, SVG-OM1)


History of Present Illness:
67 M with CAD s/p angioplasty 1980 p/w exertional dyspnea and
right soulder pain. Pt notes that he awoke in AM with right
shoulder pain and felt it could be due to the heart and so he
came in the emergency department of Union Hospital Of Cecil County Hospital. He had
associated diaphoresis with the exertional dyspnea but denied
syncope, dizziness, edema, orthopnea, or PND.  He denies
f/c/abdominal pain, n/v, constipation, diarrhea, or weight loss.
.
At Union Hospital Of Cecil County Hospital, the patient was afebrile, hypertensive
(190/106), nontachycardic (HR 60) and saturating 96% on room
air. No pathologic findings were noted on physical exam, a chest
radiograph was normal, and cardiac markers were not elevated.
However, his ECG showed lateral T wave inversions and [**Street Address(2) 101404**] elevation in lead III as well as S waves in the
precordium.
.
The was ruled out for MI by enzymes. A stress test with myoview
was performed, which showed a moderate size region of reversible
perfusion defect consistent with ischemia of the lateral and
infero-lateral wall. The patient was noted to have an EF of 42%
and inferior hypokinesis on wall motion analysis.
.
HCTZ were begun in addition to the patients BB. Transfer summary
notes that the patient was not started on an ACE-i because his
EF was not reduced.
.
Cardiac cath at Baystate Mary Lane Hospital on 2012-03-04 showed left main and 3-vessel
CAD.  Eval for surgery.

Past Medical History:
CAD s/p MI and angioplasty 1980
R shoulder surgery s/p humerus injury

Social History:
Partially retired plumber
No tobacco
No EtOH


Family History:
NC

Physical Exam:
T97.3F, HR 57, BP 120/87, RR 18, SaO2 95%,RA.
RRR without M or S3/S4.
CTAB without rales or wheezes
Abdomen soft, NT/ND, no HSM.
No extremity edema. 2+ pulses x 4 extremities.
Skin warm and dry.

Pertinent Results:
Union Hospital Of Cecil County Hospital:
-CXR: No acute cardiopulmonary process.
-Myoview SPECT scan with wall motion studies and EF: Left
ventricel normal size and configuration; moderate sized region
of reversible ischmia involving lateral/inferolateral wall. No
fixed perfusion abnormalities. Mild septal and inferior
hypokinesis; EF 42%.
-ECG: ST elevations in leads III and avF; septal R waves; V4-V5
T wave inversions, V3 biphasic T wave., I and avL with ST
depressions.
-CHOL-167 TG-74 HDL-32 LDL-120
-TrI<0.1, CK-86, CK-MB-1.1
.
Baystate Mary Lane Hospital laboratories:
2012-03-04 05:20PM   WBC-5.2 HCT-37.9* MCV-82 RDW-13.6 PLT-229
2012-03-04 05:20PM   NEUTS-40.2* LYMPHS-53.1* MONOS-4.7 EOS-1.6
BASOS-0.5
2012-03-04 05:20PM   PT-21.8* PTT-79.2* INR(PT)-3.1
2012-03-04 06:53PM   PT-12.9  PTT-32.7  INR(PT)-1.1.
2012-03-04 05:20PM   SODIUM-131*    CHLORIDE-100   UREA N-17
GLUCOSE-92
                   POTASSIUM-3.5  TOTAL CO2-22   CREAT-0.8
ANION GAP-9
                   ALT(SGPT)-32   AST(SGOT)-23   CK(CPK)-81
ALK PHOS-34*
                   TOT BILI-0.6   DIR BILI-0.2   INDIR BIL-0.4
                   AMYLASE-71     ALBUMIN-3.6
.
Baystate Mary Lane Hospital ECG:
Left axis with ST elevations in III and F, ST depresions in I,
avL; j-point elevation in V2-V3, small r-wave in V1, delayed
r-wave progression with lateral V4-V5 ST-segment depressions and
TWI.
.
Baystate Mary Lane Hospital Cardiac catheterization:
-FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
1. Three vessel coronary artery disease.
2. Mild systolic and diastolic ventricular dysfunction.
-COMMENTS:
1. Selective coronary angiography revealed a right-dominant
system. The LMCA had a 50% distal stenosis involving the origin
of the LAD and LCx. The LAD had a 60% ostial stenosis and
diffuse disease throughout. The Lcx 60% ostial stenosis and 80%
proximal stenosis just before the bifurcation of 2 large OMs.
The RCA had severe diffuse disease with a 60% stenosis
proximally and 70% before the PDA.
2. Left ventriculography revealed an estimated ejection fraction
of 55% with posterobasal HK. There was no mitral regurgitation.
3. Central hemodynamics revealed a mildly elevated left-sided
filling pressure (LVEDP 17mmHg). There was severe central
arterial hypertension systolic 197mmHg).


Brief Hospital Course:
67 M with CAD s/p angioplasty 1980 p/w exertional dyspnea and
right soulder pain. Pt notes that he awoke in AM with right
shoulder pain and felt it could be due to the heart and so he
came in the emergency department of Union Hospital Of Cecil County Hospital. He had
associated diaphoresis with the exertional dyspnea but denied
syncope, dizziness, edema, orthopnea, or PND.  He denies
f/c/abdominal pain, n/v, constipation, diarrhea, or weight loss.
.
At Union Hospital Of Cecil County Hospital, the patient was afebrile, hypertensive
(190/106), nontachycardic (HR 60) and saturating 96% on room
air. No pathologic findings were noted on physical exam, a chest
radiograph was normal, and cardiac markers were not elevated.
However, his ECG showed lateral T wave inversions and [**Street Address(2) 101404**] elevation in lead III as well as S waves in the
precordium.
.
The was ruled out for MI by enzymes. A stress test with myoview
was performed, which showed a moderate size region of reversible
perfusion defect consistent with ischemia of the lateral and
infero-lateral wall. The patient was noted to have an EF of 42%
and inferior hypokinesis on wall motion analysis.
.
HCTZ were begun in addition to the patients BB. Transfer summary
notes that the patient was not started on an ACE-i because his
EF was not reduced.
.
Cardiac cath at Baystate Mary Lane Hospital on 2012-03-04 showed left main and 3-vessel
CAD.  Eval for surgery.

Pt was taken to the OR 2000-02-07 for CABG x3 (LIMA-LAD, SVG-PDA,
SVG-OM1).  For more detailed account, see operative note.
Post-op, he was transferred the CSRU where he was extubated on
POD 0, chest tubes were removed on POD 1, and pericardial wires
were removed on POD 2.  He was transferred to the floor on POD 2
where he did well.  Cleared PT on 2003-12-17.

Medications on Admission:
ALLERGIES: Lipitor (myositis)

MEDS at home:
EC-ASA 325 mg po daily
Metoprolol XL 50 mg po daily
.
.
.
MEDS on transfer:
Tylenol 650mg q4-6 hrs prn
Percocet 5-325mg, 1-2 tabs po q4-6 h prn shoulder pain
Ambien 10 mg po qhs
HCTZ 12.5mg po daily
EC-ASA 325 mg po daily
Metoprolol XL 50 mg po daily

Discharge Medications:
1. Docusate Sodium 100 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO BID (2
times a day).
Disp:*60 Capsule(s)* Refills:*0*
2. Aspirin 81 mg Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) Sig: One (1)
Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) PO DAILY (Daily).
Disp:*30 Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.)(s)* Refills:*2*
3. Metoprolol Tartrate 25 mg Tablet Sig: Three (3) Tablet PO BID

Disp:*180 Tablet(s)* Refills:*2*
4. Pantoprazole Sodium 40 mg Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) Sig:
One (1) Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) PO Q24H (every 24 hours).
Disp:*30 Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.)(s)* Refills:*2*
5. Hydromorphone HCl 2 mg Tablet Sig: 1-2 Tablets PO Q4-6H
(every 4 to 6 hours) as needed.
Disp:*40 Tablet(s)* Refills:*0*
6. Ferrous Sulfate 325 (65) mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO
DAILY (Daily).
Disp:*30 Tablet(s)* Refills:*2*
7. Ascorbic Acid 500 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO BID (2
times a day).
Disp:*60 Tablet(s)* Refills:*2*


Discharge Disposition:
Home With Service

Facility:
Via Christi Rehabilitation Hospital Inc VNA

Discharge Diagnosis:
1. CAD
2. HTN


Discharge Condition:
Good

Discharge Instructions:
1. Medications as directed.
2. Call office or go to ER if fever/chills, discharge from
surgical sites, chest pain, shortness of breath.

Followup Instructions:
PCP, 2 weeks, please call for appointment.

Cardiologist, 2 weeks, please call for appointment.

Dr. Garner, 4 weeks, please call for appointment.
